title: "Mom, I saved someone from Rose Day!"
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman frames Oklahoma's annual Rose Day as a pro-life PR ritual that actually obstructs the abolition of abortion, then celebrates a teenager who persuaded a friend to skip Rose Day in favor of Abolition Day and the SB13 push.
audiences: Pro-life Christian considering attending Rose Day
Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 versus regulatory bills
Abolitionist activist doing one-on-one conversion of pro-lifers
Skeptic who sees abolitionists as needlessly divisive
Parent or youth pastor discipling teenagers on this issue

questions.question: What is Rose Day and what does it actually accomplish for preborn children?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian considering attending Rose Day
questions.answer: Rose Day is the annual Oklahoma event where pro-life leaders deliver roses to politicians and promote bills that regulate abortion rather than abolish it. Silberman argues none of these bills end the murder of preborn human beings; they merely codify the conditions under which it remains legal.
questions.quote: Every year, the pro-life leaders in Oklahoma put on an event to deliver roses to pro-life politicians and tout the pro-life bills on the agenda for the current session. These bills all regulate the practice of murdering preborn human beings in Oklahoma. None of them abolish abortion.

questions.question: How do pro-life regulatory bills function as obstacles to abolition rather than steps toward it?
questions.audience: Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 versus regulatory bills
questions.answer: According to Silberman, politicians and lobbyists actively use Rose Day bills as the rationale for refusing to take up SB13. He cites Tony Lauinger's letter to Senator Julie Daniels coaching her on passing two regulatory bills while killing the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.
questions.quote: Not only do they not abolish abortion, these bills are used by pro-life politicians and lobbyists as the excuse  not to abolish abortion. Last session, Oklahomans for Life President Tony Lauinger wrote a letter to State Senator Julie Daniels advising her on how to pass two “Rose Day bills” while killing the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.

questions.question: What historical precedent do abolitionists invoke to condemn regulatory "compromise" schemes?
questions.audience: Skeptic who sees abolitionists as needlessly divisive
questions.answer: Silberman draws a direct line to William Lloyd Garrison and the 1833 Declaration of Sentiments, which rejected any scheme that pretended to aid emancipation while substituting for immediate, total abolition of slavery.
questions.quote: “We regard as delusive, cruel and dangerous, any scheme of expatriation which pretends to aid, either directly or indirectly, in the emancipation of the slaves, or to be a substitute for the immediate and total abolition of slavery.”

questions.question: What does Silberman say pro-life politicians actually want to do with SB13?
questions.audience: Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 versus regulatory bills
questions.answer: He charges that pro-life politicians want SB13 dead and replaced with weak regulations specifying who may kill a preborn human and how. He calls this an ongoing game in which Rose Day organizers are knowing participants.
questions.quote: Pro-life politicians do not want to abolish abortion. They want to kill the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) and instead pass weak, pro-life bills specifying how, when, where, and by whom a preborn human can be murdered.

questions.question: What concrete sign of grassroots conversion does the article highlight?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist doing one-on-one conversion of pro-lifers
questions.answer: Silberman shares a Facebook message from Bartlesville mom Jennifer Keener whose 17-year-old daughter Hailey told a coworker the difference between abolition and Rose Day, persuading the friend to attend the Capitol on February 11 instead.
questions.quote: “My 17 year old daughter met me this morning with a story from work last night. She exclaimed, ‘I saved someone from Rose Day!’ She helped a friend see the difference between Abolishing abortion and Rose Day and her friend is now making plans to attend the Capitol February 11. 😊“

questions.question: Where does Silberman trace the origin of today's no-compromise abolition movement?
questions.audience: Parent or youth pastor discipling teenagers on this issue
questions.answer: He credits the Abolitionist Society of Norman, founded in 2010, as the seed of the movement, and frames the current period as exponential growth where ordinary Christians like Hailey are becoming the norm in Oklahoma.
questions.quote: The movement to reject compromise in favor of totally and immediately abolishing abortion started with the Abolitionist Society of Norman back in 2010. Thanks to faithful abolitionists who have long had their hand to the plow, we’re now in a period of exponential growth in the movement to the point where women like Jennifer and her daughter Hailey are becoming the norm in Oklahoma.

questions.question: What action does Silberman ask readers to take regarding friends planning to attend Rose Day?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist doing one-on-one conversion of pro-lifers
questions.answer: He asks readers to imitate Hailey by sending the article to anyone planning to attend Rose Day and urging them to redirect to Abolition Day, where Christians demand total and immediate abolition.
questions.quote: If you know anybody planning on going to Rose Day this year, be like Hailey. Send them this article and exhort them to instead attend Abolition Day, the rally where the Christians of Oklahoma join together to call for an end to the cruel and delusive regulatory schemes of the pro-life politicians and lobbyists and demand abortion’s total and immediate abolition.

related.title: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Provides the underlying Tony Lauinger letter that this article references as evidence pro-life leaders use Rose Day bills to kill SB13.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Names the Oklahoma pro-life establishment figures, including Lauinger, who run the regulatory game Silberman attacks here.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Companion piece arguing that opposition to SB13 from pro-life voices is calculated rather than uninformed.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13" ?
related.reason: Lays out the strategic case for the abolitionist insistence on SB13 over regulatory alternatives championed at Rose Day.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Shows the broader Oklahoma church-and-politics fight over SB13 that Rose Day organizers are part of.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: Documents the kind of grassroots Christian conversion to abolition that Hailey's story exemplifies on a personal scale.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Articulates the categorical distinction between abolitionism and pro-life-ism that Hailey communicated to her coworker.
